segments.text: There are silver mines and places where gold is refined.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Iron is extracted from the earth and copper is smelted from its ore.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Miners take lamps into the darkness underground and search for ore as far as they can go in the shadows and the gloom.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They dig a mineshaft far from where people live or anyone ever goes.  They swing from ropes that hang in the pits.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Bread comes from the earth, but underneath it looks as if it has been turned upside-down by fire.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Here the rocks contain lapis lazuli and the dust contains gold.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No birds of prey can see these paths, no falcon's eye can perceive.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No wild beasts have passed that way; the lion has not walked there.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Miners attack the hard rock; they overturn the roots of mountains.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They tunnel through the rock, looking carefully for every precious stone.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They dam the sources of the rivers,  and bring to light what is hidden.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But where can wisdom be found? Where is the place to gain understanding?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Human beings do not know the way to wisdom; it is not found among the living.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The deep waters say, ‘It's not here,’ and the sea says, ‘It's not here either.’
segments.speaker: The depth

segments.text: It cannot be bought with gold; nor can it be purchased with silver.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Its value cannot be measured, even with the gold of Ophir;  it is more precious than onyx or lapis lazuli.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Gold or fine glass cannot compare with wisdom; it cannot be exchanged for gold jewelry.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Coral and crystal are not worth mentioning; the price of wisdom is far above rubies.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Topaz from Ethiopia can't compare with it; it cannot be bought with the purest gold.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So where does wisdom come from? Where is the place to gain understanding?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Wisdom is hidden from the sight of all living things, even the birds of the air cannot see it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Abaddon and Death say, ‘We've only heard a rumor of it.’
segments.speaker: Destruction and death

segments.text: Only God understands the path to wisdom; he knows where it is found.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For he looks to the very end of the earth; he sees everything under heaven.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He decided how strong the wind should blow, and regulated the waters.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He set a limit for the rain and made a path for the lightning.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then he considered wisdom. He examined it, gave it his approval, and declared it good.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He said to humankind, ‘To reverence the Lord is wisdom, and to turn away from evil is understanding.’
segments.speaker: God
